content.problemStatement:
Chef works as a cook in a restaurant. Each morning, he has to drive down a straight road with length KKK to reach the restaurant from his home. Let's describe this road using a coordinate XXX; the position of Chef's home is X=0X = 0X=0 and the position of the restaurant is X=KX = KX=K.

The road has exactly two lanes (numbered 111 and 222), but there are NNN obstacles (numbered 111 through NNN) on it. For each valid iii, the iii-th obstacle blocks the lane LiL_iLi​ at the position X=XiX = X_iX=Xi​ and does not block the other lane.

When driving, Chef cannot pass through an obstacle. He can switch lanes in zero time at any integer XXX-coordinate which does not coincide with the XXX-coordinate of any obstacle. However, whenever he switches lanes, he cannot switch again until driving for at least DDD units of distance, and he can travel only in the direction of increasing XXX. Chef can start driving in any lane he wants. He can not switch lanes at non-integer  XXX-coordinate.

Sometimes, it is impossible to reach the restaurant without stopping at an obstacle. Find the maximum possible distance Chef can travel before he has to reach an obstacle which is in the same lane as him. If he can avoid all obstacles and reach the restaurant, the answer is KKK.
constraints: 1≤Xi≤K−11 \le X_i \le K-11≤Xi​≤K−1 for each valid iii
Xi<Xi+1X_i \lt X_{i+1}Xi​<Xi+1​ for each valid iii
the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 10610^6106
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains three space-separated integers NNN, KKK and DDD.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers X1,X2,…,XNX_1, X_2, \ldots, X_NX1​,X2​,…,XN​.
The third line contains NNN space-separated integers L1,L2,…,LNL_1, L_2, \ldots, L_NL1​,L2​,…,LN​.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer ― the maximum distance Chef can travel.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1: Chef can start in lane 222 and switch to lane 111 at the position X=6X = 6X=6, then continue until reaching the restaurant.
Example case 2: Chef can start in lane 222 and switch to lane 111 at X=5X = 5X=5. However, he cannot avoid the obstacle at X=13X = 13X=13, because he has not driven for at least 202020 units since the last switch.
Example case 3: Chef should start in lane 222 and then switch lanes at the positions X=2X=2X=2, X=4X=4X=4, X=6X=6X=6 and X=8X=8X=8. This way, he can reach the restaurant.
Example case 4: Chef can start in lane 222 but he cannot escape the second obstacle at XXX=5 since the first obstacle at XXX=4 doesn't give enough space for Chef to switch lanes.
